name: AI Engineer Pack
description: Workflows for coding agents building AI-powered features: prompt engineering, LLM integration, evaluation harnesses, AI-aware observability, and safe output handling.

codex.notes: Skills and portable managed AI prompts, templates, tools, and memory validate and install through dry-run planning.

claude-code.notes: Agent Skills and portable managed capabilities are compatible; no Claude-specific plugin wrapper is required.

capabilities.name: AI engineer pack guidance
capabilities.content: Use the AI Engineer Pack to move from AI feature idea to production-ready implementation. First clarify the user outcome, model behavior, safety boundary, data access, latency/cost target, evaluation plan, observability needs, and rollback path. Prefer evidence-backed plans with explicit failure modes, test data, acceptance criteria, and owner/date follow-ups.

capabilities.name: AI product requirements review
capabilities.content: Review this AI feature proposal. Identify the user job, model task, input/output contract, safety and privacy boundaries, tool/data access, expected failure modes, fallback behavior, latency/cost constraints, evaluation strategy, observability signals, and launch gates. End with decisions needed and owner/date actions.

capabilities.name: Prompt change regression review
capabilities.content: Review this prompt or system-instruction change for behavior drift. Compare intended behavior, likely regressions, jailbreak or prompt-injection exposure, output-format stability, tool-use side effects, privacy risk, and missing eval cases. Recommend whether to merge, test more, or redesign.

capabilities.name: Model selection review
capabilities.content: Evaluate model choices for this AI feature. Compare capability fit, latency, cost, reliability, privacy/data-retention constraints, tool support, streaming requirements, fallback strategy, and eval evidence. Recommend a primary model, fallback model, and launch monitoring plan.
